title: Unit 11 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Future Tenses (Θα) and Aspect

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 11: Future Tenses and Aspect. In Unit 10, we learned the magic of 'Aspect'... that choice between the Video and the Photo. Now... we are going to apply that exact same logic to the future. I'm William... and here with me is Athina. Athina... how do we say 'will' in Greek?
In Greek, the future is incredibly simple. We use one tiny magic word:
Just put it right in front of your verb.
No new endings to learn... just a prefix. But... remember the 'Pronoun Sandwich' rule. If you want to say 'I will do IT'... you have to sandwich the pronoun between 'Tha' and the verb. Right, Athina?
Θα το κάνω. Θα σου το δώσω...
I will give it to you. The
loves to stay close to the verb, but the short pronouns are the only things allowed to jump in the middle.
A little pronoun filling! Now... let’s look at our two lenses again.
I will be reading every day... that is a habit, a 'Video'. But...
I will read the book tomorrow... just once. That is a snapshot, a 'Photo'.

90% of your daily conversation will use that 'Photo' lens... the Simple Future. But beware the 'Augment Trap'! We haven't learned the Past yet... but many students mistakenly add an 'e' sound to the future. Is that allowed?
NEVER! The 'e-' sound... the augment... is strictly for the past. If you say...
it sounds completely wrong. It must be...
Got it. No 'e' in the future. Now... negation. In Unit 10, we learned that
is a regular statement about the future, so it stays with our old friend
Δεν θα παίξω. Δεν θα σου το δώσω.
Final masterclass secret: The Probability Trick. Greeks love to use
even when we aren't talking about the future! Athina?
to mean 'must be' or 'probably'. If someone knocks on the door, we say:
It must be George! Or if someone is late:

is the future... AND the guess. You’ve now mastered the Future tense and reinforced the power of Aspect. You can now plan... promise... and suppose. Excellent work... and see you in Unit 12... where we finally learn how to build those Simple stems.
Συγχαρητήρια! Τα λέμε στο επόμενο μάθημα.
